description: Diagnostic decision tree for A2442 (MacBook Pro 14" M1 Pro/Max base, J314-BTR). Follow sequentially from Step 1. Pro/Max topology: has Viper VR (GPU), Monaco VR (CPU cluster), MagSafe, 3x USB-C, HDMI, SD card. Dual SSD (SSD0+SSD1). Charger is MagSafe or USB-C.
WARNING: A2442 (820-02098) MBP 14" M1 Pro/Max ONLY. SVR AON is 42A design (NOT 30A like base M1/M2). Comparator is summing amplifier circuit (NOT simple U8110). BOM-selectable thresholds: 30A (BOM1) vs 42A (BOM2). Has Viper VR and Monaco VR — NOT present on base M1/M2 boards. Similar to CTO variant 820-02443.
data_source: Schematic PNGs cross-referenced with page_index. Power-on sequence verified against Eason (iphone_cpu_fix) M1 standby circuit reference PDF. Pro/Max architecture with additional VRs for dedicated CPU/GPU power.

eason_reference.source: M1 series standby circuit and power-on principle, and 20V boost circuit (Eason @ iphone_cpu_fix)
power_on_sequence: 1. PPVBUS_USBC or MagSafe 5V → CD3217/CD3218 MOSFET → PPHV_INT_AONSW → fuses → PPDCIN_AONSW
2. PPDCIN_AONSW → R5220 → PPDCIN_AON_CHGR_R → U5200 (power). R5215/R5216 → CHGR_AUX_DET → U5200 (detect)
3. U5200 → gate drives → PPBUS_AON (12.6V temp). CHGR_EN_MVR → delay circuit → P3V8AON_PWR_EN → U5700

5. PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN → U8100(MPMU) + summing amplifier comparator (page 36). AON Output Throttle circuit — NOT simple U8110. BOM-selectable: 30A (BOM1) vs 42A (BOM2). When threshold met, PMU_VDD_HI → U8100 voltage detect
6. U8100 → all MPMU rails. PMIC Master (pages 42-44). U7700 → SPMU rails. PMIC Slave (pages 38-40)
7. SoC hard start (24MHz + Y8301 32.768KHz + PMU_RESET_L). SoC reads ROM, I2C to CD3217/CD3218
8. SoC → U8100 → P5VS2_PWR_EN → PP5V_S2 (page 59, AFTER hard start)
9. Viper VR (pages 47-51) → GPU dedicated power. Monaco VR (pages 52-58) → CPU cluster power
10. CD3217/CD3218 reads shared ROM, PD via CC → charger boosts to 20V. 20V → PPDCIN_AONSW → U5200 → real PPBUS_AON (12.6V)

forced_start_bypass.description: Bypass protective isolation to test if fault is pre-SVR or post-SVR. On Pro/Max, the comparator is a summing amplifier circuit, not a simple IC like U8110.
steps: 1. Connect PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN to 3.8V externally (4.3V if testing with display)
2. Force the summing amplifier comparator output high at the comparator output stage on page 36 (NOT a simple U8110 pin jumper — Pro/Max uses summing amplifier circuit with BOM-selectable thresholds)
3. Press start button OR connect charger for 3 seconds

interpretation.forced_start_works: Fault is FRONT END — CD3217/CD3218, MagSafe controller, charger IC, fuses, boost circuit. Check Steps 1-2.
interpretation.forced_start_fails: Fault is BACK END — PMU, SoC, Viper/Monaco VRs, clocks, ROM. Check Steps 3+.

diagnostic_shortcuts.name: PPVAON_FAULT_L
diagnostic_shortcuts.description: Active low fault from SVR AON U5700. If low, U5700 reports a fault condition — check phase drivers and overcurrent.

diagnostic_shortcuts.name: PMU_ACTIVE_READY
diagnostic_shortcuts.description: MPMU U8100 output indicating PMU is alive and has completed initialization. If missing, PMU never started.

diagnostic_shortcuts.name: 32.768kHz crystal
diagnostic_shortcuts.description: Y8301 crystal required for PMU startup. If dead or not oscillating, U8100 will not start.
diagnostic_shortcuts.probe: Y8301 oscillator pins — check for 32.768kHz with scope

diagnostic_shortcuts.name: SOC_FORCE_DFU
diagnostic_shortcuts.description: Force DFU mode to bypass normal boot and test if SoC is responsive at all.
diagnostic_shortcuts.probe: DFU test point or via USB-C connection with Apple Configurator

diagnostic_shortcuts.name: Phase balance (RS710/RS711/RS712)
diagnostic_shortcuts.description: Check current sense resistors for all 3 SVR AON phases. Imbalanced phases indicate a dead phase or shorted FET.
diagnostic_shortcuts.probe: RS710 (Phase 1), RS711 (Phase 2), RS712 (Phase 3) — voltage drop across each should be similar

diagnostic_shortcuts.name: CD3217 SYS_DETECT
diagnostic_shortcuts.description: Battery presence detection signal. Without SYS_DETECT, charger IC will not enable PPBUS_AON.
diagnostic_shortcuts.probe: SYS_DETECT from battery connector — check Q5155 MOSFET gate/drain

diagnostic_shortcuts.name: PP5V_S2 bootstrap (R5900)
diagnostic_shortcuts.description: PP5V_S2 enable is gated by PMU GPIO after SoC hard start. R5900 area bootstrap circuit.

diagnostic_shortcuts.name: BOM threshold check (30A vs 42A)
diagnostic_shortcuts.description: Pro/Max specific: summing amplifier on page 36 has BOM-selectable thresholds. BOM1=30A, BOM2=42A. Wrong BOM population causes premature or delayed PMU enable.
diagnostic_shortcuts.probe: AON Output Throttle page 36 — check resistor values in summing amplifier feedback network

diagnostic_shortcuts.name: Viper VR fault check
diagnostic_shortcuts.description: Viper VR provides dedicated GPU power (pages 47-51). Not present on base M1/M2 boards. Check VIPER_FAULT_L signal.
diagnostic_shortcuts.probe: Viper VR fault line and power stage outputs (pages 47-51)

diagnostic_shortcuts.name: Monaco VR fault check
diagnostic_shortcuts.description: Monaco VR provides dedicated CPU cluster power (pages 52-58). Not present on base boards. Check MONACO_FAULT/ALERT signals.
diagnostic_shortcuts.probe: Monaco VR CLVR GPIO alert signals and output caps (pages 52-58)

tree.probe: Bulk capacitors near U5200 charger IC output (page 29, Charger/PBUS). C52xx area.

no.diagnosis: Charger path fault — check both MagSafe and USB-C input paths
check_sequence: Check MagSafe input: J5401 MagSafe connector (page 31), MagSafe controller (page 32), MagSafe support (page 33)
Check USB-C input: PPVBUS_USBC at USB-C connectors — Left ATC0/ATC1 (pages 72-81), Right ATC2 (pages 82-90)
Check CD3217/CD3218 port controllers on each USB-C port
Check SYS_DETECT from battery connector — charger needs battery detect signal
Check charger IC U5200 (page 29) gate drives
Check fuses upstream of charger IC
Check MagSafe path separately: if MagSafe charges but USB-C doesn't (or vice versa), isolate the failing path
Thermal cam on charger area for shorted FETs

tree.probe: PP3V8_AON output caps near U5700 (SoC Power pages 15-22). B5700/CS7xx area.

no.diagnosis: SVR AON (U5700) fault — 42A ICC MAX design, 3-phase
check_sequence: Check P3V8AON_PWR_EN enable signal
Check PPVAON_FAULT_L — if low, U5700 reports fault
Check AON Output Throttle summing amplifier circuit (page 36) — Pro/Max uses summing amplifier, NOT simple U8110
Check BOM threshold: BOM1=30A vs BOM2=42A — wrong population causes issues
Check phase balance: RS710/RS711/RS712 sense resistors — voltage drop should be equal across all 3 phases
Check 3-phase driver FETs: Q5800 (DRVL1 via L5800), Q5820 (DRVL2 via L5820), Q5840 (DRVL3 via L5840)
Thermal cam on U5700 and phase FETs — 42A design runs significantly hotter than 30A base boards
SHORTCUT: Try forced start bypass — inject 3.8V on PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN + force summing amplifier output high (page 36) + press start. If board boots, fault is upstream of SVR.

tree.probe: AON LDO output caps — UC100 for PP3V3_AON, UC120 for PP1V8_AON

check_sequence: Check PP3V8_AON input to LDOs — if missing, fault is upstream at U5700
If PP3V3_AON missing: check UC100 input/output, diode mode on output cap
If PP1V8_AON missing: check UC120 input/output, diode mode on output cap
Thermal cam on LDO area

tree.probe: Slave PMU buck output caps (pages 38-40). VIN/LDO/Bucks/GPIO sections.
tree.expect: Multiple buck rails present — DRAM, NAND, and S1/S2 domain rails

check_sequence: Check PP3V8_AON input to U7700 (page 38, PMIC Slave VIN)
Check PPBUS_AON input to SPMU
Check individual BUCK outputs via pages 38-40
Check SPMU_READY / SPMU_ACTIVE_READY signal
Diode mode across output cap banks for downstream shorts
Thermal cam on U7700 area
If single buck missing: find downstream short on that rail before replacing PMU

tree.probe: Master PMU buck output caps (pages 42-44). VIN/LDO/Bucks/GPIO sections. PMU Support pages 45-46.
tree.expect: Multiple buck rails present — PCPU, GPU, SOC domain rails. PMU_ACTIVE_READY high.

check_sequence: Check PP3V8_AON_MPMU input to U8100 (page 42, PMIC Master VIN)

CRITICAL: Check summing amplifier comparator (page 36) — PPBUS_AON via voltage divider to comparator. PP3V8_AON to opposite input. Comparator output drives PMU_VDD_HI to U8100. Without PMU_VDD_HI, U8100 will NOT start. Pro/Max uses summing amplifier, not simple U8110.
Check Y8301 crystal (32.768KHz) — U8100 needs this clock to start
Check PMU_ONOFF_L — pulled up by PP1V8_AON_MPMU
Check PMU_ACTIVE_READY output (pages 42-44)
Check PMU Support circuits (pages 45-46)
Check power button circuit
Thermal cam on U8100 and surrounding area
Check individual buck and LDO outputs across pages 42-44
Check P5VS2_PWR_EN enable signal — gated by PMU GPIO after SoC hard start
Check PP5V_S2 bootstrap circuit R5900 area (page 59)
Check VR input PPBUS_AON and output inductor
Check PP5V_S2 power good signal
Diode mode on output caps for downstream shorts
NOTE: PP5V_S2 only comes up AFTER SoC hard start — if SoC never starts, this rail will be absent

tree.probe: Viper VR controller and power stages (pages 47-51). PPVDD_GPU output area.
tree.expect: Variable voltage present (DVFS controlled). Viper fault line NOT asserted.
tree.note: Viper VR is Pro/Max ONLY — not present on base M1/M2 boards. Provides dedicated GPU power that base boards get from PMU bucks.

no.diagnosis: Viper VR fault — GPU has no dedicated power
check_sequence: Check VIPER_FAULT_L signal — if low, Viper reports fault condition
Check Viper BOOT signal (pages 47-51)
Check all power stage outputs across pages 47-51
Check Viper input cap banks and sense resistors
Check PP1V8_S1 monitoring regulator for Viper
Thermal cam on Viper controller and power stage MOSFETs
If Max chip: additional power stages may be populated — check Max-specific BOM options

tree.check: Monaco VR — CPU cluster dedicated power
tree.probe: Monaco VR instances (pages 52-58). PPVDD_PCPU/ECPU output areas.
tree.expect: Variable voltage present (DVFS controlled). Monaco ALERT signals NOT asserted.
tree.note: Monaco VR is Pro/Max ONLY — not present on base M1/M2 boards. Provides dedicated CPU cluster power. Multiple instances.

no.diagnosis: Monaco VR fault — CPU cluster has no dedicated power
check_sequence: Check which Monaco instance is failing (pages 52-58)
Check CLVR GPIO connections on each instance for ALERT signals
Check Monaco input caps on each instance
If Max chip: additional Monaco instances may be populated for Max-specific CPU clusters
Check CLVR fabric throttle signal
Thermal cam on Monaco controllers and output stages
Check Reference Design Overrides for Max-specific Monaco instances

tree.probe: SoC Power pages 15-22. PCPU from Viper VR, GPU from Viper, CPU clusters from Monaco. DDR/SRAM from PMU bucks.
tree.expect: Multiple voltage domains present. Board should attempt boot.

no.diagnosis: SoC fault, SPI ROM fault, or power domain issue
check_sequence: Check SPI NOR flash (SoC Power pages 15-22)
Check secure element
Check SoC ground connections
Check NAND/storage power — dual SSD: SSD0 + SSD1 (pages 102-112)
Verify Viper VR (Step 6.5) and Monaco VR (Step 6.6) outputs are present at SoC pins
If all power present but no boot: try DFU mode via USB-C (SOC_FORCE_DFU shortcut)

substeps.probe: Display power pages 114-116. Luxe backlight controller. Display connector.
substeps.note: A2442 uses Luxe backlight (mini-LED). More complex than older backlight ICs.

substeps.probe: MagSafe connector J5401 (page 31), MagSafe controller (page 32), MagSafe support (page 33), Charger/PBUS (page 29)
substeps.note: Pro/Max has MagSafe — NOT present on base M1 Air. Check MagSafe CC detect, temperature sense, and power path independently from USB-C.

substeps.probe: Left ATC0/ATC1 (pages 72-81), Right ATC2 (pages 82-90). CD3217/CD3218 controllers on each port.
substeps.note: 3 USB-C ports (2 left, 1 right). Each has independent CD3217/CD3218, SPI ROM, and PD controller. Test each port independently.

substeps.probe: Madea controller UK700 (pages 91-95). HDMI connector and DP-to-HDMI conversion.
substeps.note: Pro/Max specific — Madea handles DisplayPort to HDMI conversion. Check 3.3V LDO and 1.2V VR for Madea.

substeps.probe: SD card controller and support (pages 96-99). GL9755 or equivalent.
substeps.note: Pro/Max specific. Check SD card slot connector, 3.3V supply, and PCIe link to SoC.

substeps.probe: Dual SSD: SSD0 (pages 102-107), SSD1 (pages 108-112). Ocarina controllers.
substeps.note: Pro/Max has DUAL SSD channels (SSD0+SSD1) — base M1/M2 only has SSD0. Both must be functional for full storage. NAND is paired to SoC.

substeps.probe: Audio codec and amplifiers (pages 120-124). Speaker connectors.
substeps.note: Pro/Max 14" has 6-speaker system with force-cancelling woofers.

substeps.probe: Keyboard controller and matrix (pages 126-127). IOX I2C expanders.
substeps.note: Full keyboard with function row (no Touch Bar on Pro/Max 14").
Force Touch trackpad. Check SPI TPAD lines and LDO power.

substeps.probe: TouchID sensor and secure element interface (pages 130-131).
substeps.note: Integrated in power button. Check TouchID SPI lines and PP3V3_AON supply.
